line: ...to you!
Well, that was just weird. Why did we only sing the last two words? What happened to the "Happy Birthday" part?
You know Troy is Jehovah's Witness, they don't celebrate birthdays.
Annie and I did our best to keep the language on the cake compliant.
Hello During a Random Dessert. The month and day of which coincide numerically with your expulsion from a uterus. You guys. I never cry, but...
All right, happy expulsion, Troy, but after cake, we cram, for realsies. Finals are coming up.
Yeah, this group is starting to use special occasions to avoid studying. Last week we had fondue and played Boggle because Shirley's niece took her first bath.
With bubbles.
Thank you. It's a milestone.
Funny, because my birthday was last week and nobody noticed. Or cared.
Pierce...you don't remember the huge party we threw you? We need to talk about those painkillers.
Gotcha! I remember my party, stupid! That was some party.
You still owe me for the keg deposit.
You think I don't know that?
Troy.
Troy.
Kickpuncher: Detroit
Not everywhere. I have a cousin in Detroit, they're not crazy about it there.
This is how you turn twenty. Thanks.
Here, let me help...
I broke my legs, not my gender.
While we're... watching this unfold, some birthday facts to enjoy: Troy's birthday is tomorrow, December 4th. Also born that day: Tyra Banks, Marissa Tomei and French cinematographer Claude Renoir.
Jackpot!
On the Chinese calendar, Troy is a horse, like me: Purposeful, self-possessed and gregarious.
No, I’m pretty sure I'm a snake. I remember, because I'm determined, self-possessed and mendacious.
Pierce, what is this, what are you doing? Explain yourself.
Who made this crappy cake?
A cake maker. At a cake store.
Well, he did it wrong.
Troy. We're 1990, we're horses.
I was born in '89.
Then...you were born twenty-one years ago.
Which makes me twenty...because everyone is ten for two years. Because fifth grade is really hard...for everyone. MOM! How many lies am I living?!
Do you understand, at midnight, you turn twenty-one. As in drinking age?
Whoa!
Okay, this party just became unacceptable. We’re going out.
Yep.
You guys were just complaining about us having too many parties.
Too many lame ones. This is real.
What makes it real?
Shirley, think of it as Troy taking his first bath, only the bubbles are his manhood.
I want to bathe in manhood!
I’m taking you to Johnny's.
Ucch. You mean Douchey's?
Oh, of course you hate cool bars. I don't suppose you’ve even been to Johnny's.
Hey, I've never been to Beirut, either,...And I...probably will go there, one day, because I'm sure it has a lot of interesting culture, unlike Johnny's, which is douchey!
Let's hear your suggestion.
The Red Door.
Oooooh, the Red Hipster?
It's not hipster. Hipsters haven't even found it, it's underground.
Why don't we go somewhere fun, like Peg Leg Pablo's. They have virgin mudslides.
They're milkshakes, Shirley.
You guys, all you think about is yourselves. Think about me. I'm nineteen, I can't get into a bar.
Well...
Okay, well...have fun...
Okay, I’ll get Annie an ID.
A fake ID?
It'll be real, it just won't be yours. Meet you at the Red Door.
Nice try. We’re not going to Red Poet's Society.
Not going to Douchey Cologne's.
Fine, on the count of three, name the least offensive bar you’ve ever been to, one, two, three...
Flannahan's Hole.
Done. Troy, you riding with me?
You have to ask?
Pierce, you want to come in my van?
I'm not a disabled, all right? I don't need people's help to do normal things, why can't everybody stop doting on me and leave me...Good. I'll beat you there!
I don't think this girl looks very much like me.
She's a white brunette.
So is Anne Hathaway.
Go on...
And what is your friend doing with a stack of other people's IDs?
Don't know. Sometimes people sell their ID when they're leaving the state and need cash.
So this girl's a drifter. A floater. An urchin. Caroline Decker. 20067 Moonfish Lane, Corpus Christi, Texas ...Texas? Do I need an accent?
You don't need an accent.
I'm Caroline Decker.
Don't do that.
I guar-un-tee.
That's Cajun... (on phone) What?
Flannahan's Hole is closed. That’s not... I'm not being clever, I mean it's out of business.
Well, that's what they get for trying to please everyone. So, now what?
There's a place on Third and Water. It's fun divey but not staph infection divey, it's got either a really gross name or an ironically fancy one, possibly both...
Oh.
Go to Third and Water.
Wait, why? That wasn't the deal.
Shirley's pushing back.
Why, because they don’t have plastic menus and milkshakes?
Why, because they...
Don't repeat it, you goon! Look, just... the women are your problem, the men are going to The Ballroom. And now I guess I am being clever.
So, plastic menus are bad? See, this is the kind of stuff I need to learn, plastic menus seem like a great idea to me. For when you spill something on them.
You're entering a new chapter of your life, Troy. Sadly, it's the final chapter, but it's also the longest, and if you play it right, the best. You and I are just two guys, now. Peers. Equals.
So awesome. Maybe later I can drive your car?
Hello, former enemy.
He's 21 at midnight. Cool?
Happy Birthday.
Caroline Decker from Corpus...
Annie, you don't need an accent.
If my ID says Texas, they'll be suspicious...
Look, they're not going to question your ID because you're a hot girl. We're good for business. The hotter you are, the more they'll risk the fine.
I don't like this place.
You've been clear about that, Shirley. Oy vey.
Howdy?
Can't be too careful.
I understand.
Hey Shirley! Welcome back.
You don't know me. Hmmm? OK?
Yeah.
This place is all right.
Yeah, it's OK.
These pictures must be of all the regulars. You think someday I'll make it up to this wall?
I don't say this often, Troy, but dream a little smaller.
Can I get you guys anything?
Oh! I'm OK.
Another Macallan's, neat.
Vodka neat four olives.
I'm waiting until midnight.
Awww.
Sweetie?
Water.
Where's that accent from?
Corpus Christi, Texas. 78418.
What are you doing here in town?
Not much, I reckon. Just...driftin'. Floatin'. Spittin' in the wind. General waywardness.
All righty.
Annie, you made it in, you don't need to be from Texas anymore.
I don't know how it works, I'm not a barfly, Britta.
They have Asteroids. Slide out.
Oh, OK. I'm going to go take a little look around.
Me too.
Hey, don't accept any drinks.
Or any invitations to the bathroom. So, what are you going to order for your first legal drink?
What should I get?
Whatever you want.
No wrong answers.
I like beer.
Don't order beer.
You've had beer.
What should I get?
What do you think you might like? I mean, it's your world now.
Follow your heart.
My Uncle Carl played a big role in my life. Taught me how to throw a football. He passed away this year. His favorite drink was a seven and seven.
Don't order that.
It's an awful drink.
You're better than that.
It's a high school drink for girls.
Actually, I'll have a root beer instead of that water.
Sure thing.
Thank you kindly. Y'all are so nice in this town. I'm Caroline. From Corpus Christi. Grew up on a trout farm. Diddly doo...
Careful. You score any higher on this thing and the Rylans are going to recruit you to fight the Ko-Dan Empire.
The Last Starfighter. Did you get the 25th anniversary Blu Ray?
Was Scorpius half Scarran / half Peacekeeper? Frell yes, I've got Starfighter on Blu Ray.
You're a fan of the sci-fi original series Farscape?
Can I buy you a drink?
You got some ID?
Very funny, punk. Get outta my way!
You need help, man?
Oh, you'd like that, wouldn't you? A little turning of the tables?
Turning of what tables?
I don't know! Leave me alone!
So, I punched her, right in the face.
Your probation officer? Didn't you get in trouble?
Hell yes, I got in trouble. Wasn't the first time, won't be the last. I'm not exactly known for my sound judgment. Back in Corpus Christi, they call me Capricious Caroline.
What's capricious mean?
It probably means I'm too busy living life to learn five dollar words! Ha, ha! Hot damn!
With aged Scotch you never use ice.
Never use ice. Got it. Why?
Destroys it. At most, what you want: two drops of spring water. Activates the flavor.
Good Lord. Do they have the rules to high maintenance poser drinking on the wall at L Street?
Do they?
Poser drinking? Hey, miss "Vodka neat, four olives?" What's that called? The Too-Cool-To-Care-Tini?
Is it?
I bet the forced starkness of that drink order turns a lot of horn-rimmed heads at the Red Door.
I can't wait to understand these arguments!
Hey y'all. Another round for ya, everybody holdin' up? I'll be back in two shakes of a rabbit's ass!
Who the hell was that?
I gotta go see a woman about the female equivalent of a horse.
Classy. Way to show Troy the ropes.
Shut up, L Street.
That woman is a hurricane.
Yeah.
Hurricanes are bad, Troy.
I know.

I always thought it's possible that the wormhole Chrichton fell through moved him across not only space, but also time, which would suggest that the Sebaceans are our future selves, and Chrichton is longing for a planet that no longer exists.
Huh. Hey, you're really nursing that beer.
Which would make sense, because what kind of wormhole would it be if it could only move you from one place to another within the same galaxy on the same day?
You know, speaking of wormholes, what do you say we use one to teleport this conversation some place a little more private?
That doesn't make any sense. Wormholes and teleportation are two different things. That's what I've been talking about this entire time.
I noticed.
Cheers.
I followed that band Phish, spelled with a P-H, and I just lived in parking lots wherever they played. I don't even like their music, just did it to see if I could do it. Guess what? I could.
So, what's now?

I don't know. Even if I planned it, plans fall off me like chicken crap off an armadillo. Annie's the one that loves plans. Not me. Annie's my friend. She goes to school here. Thinks she's got it all figured out. She wants to major in healthcare management. What does that even mean?
No idea.
I'll tell you what it means. It means a master's degree. Followed by an internship. She's got the next fifteen years of her life all mapped out, all she's gotta do now is follow it or screw it up.
Another soda?
Actually, I'll have a screwdriver. I got no place to be. What am I, Annie?
The first season when you watch it the second time, it's better than the third season. And the fourth season when you watch it the fifth time...
OK. What's you name?
Abed.
Abed. Robert.
Hey, Robert.
Abed. Would you like to have gay sex, with me?
No, thank you.
Wow! OK, so what is wrong with you that you can sit here this whole time and never pick up on the fact that a man is hitting on you?
Oh, I actually did pick up on it after a while.
You actually...and?
I really, really like talking about Farscape. It's a really good show.
Stargate is better.
Battery depleted.
Oh, man!
Hey there! What you been up to?
I was just a, praying for the poor souls in these photographs.
Oh, that nice, but you missed one! We found it in the ladies room.
Give that to me.
Don't feel bad, this makes us like you way more!
You think this is really funny?
Yeah.
Yeah, a little.
I had some bad years, with a chaser.
Come on, it's a funny picture because you act so perfect all the time. And, because you look like a zombie but it's not funny when you're sad. We're on your team! Relax, sit down. It's my birthday!
Give it to me. Give it to me. I'll see you all on Monday.
Shirley! Wait!
What?
Nothing. Get out of my way!
I can't get out of your way. I'm stuck and the chair broke...and I can't get...would you please help me? Pretty please? Thank you. Shirley.
You're welcome, jackass.
Hey, it's my birthday, and technically I'm not allowed to drink it for another 45 seconds, oh actually it'll be 30 seconds by the time I'm done saying this but can I have a 7 and 7?
It's on the house. Happy birthday, kid!
Big boy, big boy. You're my big, big boy.
Troy, I think I owe you a birthday.
I'm cool. I always wanted to drive this thing.
What? Oh, this my car? Don't crash it.
I'm not going to crash it.
Crash it, Troy.
Go to sleep, Britta. Go to sleep.
Crash his car, Troy.
This seems like a really dark chapter in our group's story.
Go to sleep, Abed.
Cool.
Hey, there's the place we should've gone to tonight.
Yeah, exactly, L Street.
That's the Red Door, stupid. Do you see a sign that says L Street?
L Street's too cool to have a sign. It's called L Street after the street it's on.
The Red Door is on L Street.
L Street has a red door. Hey!
It's the same bar?! You two have been saying one bar is lame and the other one is awesome all night and it's the same bar?!
Well, he probably goes there on Friday nights which is lame.
You wish.
Stop, just stop. I just spent the last two years thinking you that guys knew more than me about life and I just found out that you guys are just as dumb as me!
Duh doy.
Yeah. Duh doy.
Got it. Duh doy. Duh doy.
You can take me home first. I only live a few blocks from here.
You do?
Annie, this is a terrible neighborhood.
This is my apartment. Weird night, huh?
Yeah, alcohol makes people sad. It's like the Lifetime Movies of beverages.
I pretended to be a different person tonight.
Abed does that like three times a week.
I did it because I didn't want to be me. I did it because I'm not sure who I am. Admit it. We went to school for four years and you didn't even know me.

Yeah but, I know you now. You're Annie. You like puzzles and little monsters on your pencils and some guy names Mark Ruffalo. You're a fierce competitor and a sore loser. And, you expect everybody to be than who they are and you expect yourself to be better than everyone. Which is cool. Good night.
Night. Guess everyone is going to be pretty embarrassed on Monday, huh?
Nah, everyone just got a little drunk. No one did anything that bad, right?
Wait, wait, wait, wait. What are we doing?
What are we doing?
This is bad.
Yeah.
OK. I flew off the handle earlier. I apologize.
They were making out.
Abed!
Why the hell would you say that?
Why would you do it right in front of me? I'm not a coat rack.
Abed, no one likes a tattle tail.
Happy Birthday, Troy.
Thanks.
You're a man now.
I don't think this is going to be big enough.
Just try it. We're so close.
Right. Breathe in. Arms. 157!
We did it! Little help? Hurry, I think it's a girl.
Left side or right side?
Left side.
Got it. Tell me when I'm close.
OK.
